title: Real-Time Thermal Super-Resolution System
description: Machine learning system that enhances low-resolution thermal camera feeds to high-quality images at 229+ FPS for robotics and autonomous systems.
longDescription: This project introduces the first real-time thermal super-resolution framework optimized for autonomous vehicles, robotics, and industrial applications. Thermal cameras produce low-resolution images, limiting their effectiveness in critical applications like autonomous driving, industrial monitoring, and surveillance. I developed a lightweight neural network based on the Information Multi-Distillation Network (IMDN) architecture with only 0.69M parameters, making it suitable for edge deployment. The system achieves 34.2 dB PSNR (Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio) at 229+ frames per second, outperforming existing methods in both quality and speed. As the sole developer, I designed the architecture, implemented a custom thermal-aware loss function to preserve thermal characteristics during enhancement, and optimized the model for real-time inference using TensorRT. The solution includes cross-domain transfer learning to adapt RGB-pretrained models to thermal data, resulting in superior detail preservation. Built with PyTorch for training and TensorRT for deployment, this system establishes a new state-of-the-art benchmark for real-time thermal image enhancement and is suitable for applications requiring immediate thermal perception, such as autonomous navigation and safety monitoring.
problem: Thermal cameras produce low-resolution images, limiting their use in autonomous vehicles, robotics, industrial inspection, and surveillance applications that require high-quality thermal perception.
solution: Developed a lightweight neural network (IMDN architecture with 0.69M parameters) optimized for real-time thermal image enhancement with custom thermal-aware loss function and TensorRT optimization.
myRole: Sole developer - designed architecture, implemented thermal-aware training pipeline, optimized for real-time inference with TensorRT, and validated on thermal datasets.
outcome: Achieved 34.2 dB PSNR at 229+ FPS—first real-time thermal super-resolution system suitable for edge deployment in robotics and autonomous systems.
features: Real-time performance: 34.2 dB PSNR at 229+ FPS
Lightweight architecture: only 0.69M parameters for edge deployment
Custom thermal-aware multi-component loss function preserves thermal characteristics
Cross-domain RGB-to-thermal transfer learning for improved accuracy
TensorRT optimization for production deployment
Applications in autonomous driving, robotics, security, and industrial monitoring

title: Real-Time Lebanese Sign Language Translator
description: AI system that translates Lebanese Sign Language into spoken and written text in real-time using computer vision and natural language processing.
longDescription: Omnisign is an AI-driven sign language interpreter developed as a final-year project to enhance communication accessibility for the deaf and hard-of-hearing community in Lebanon. The system uses computer vision to recognize Lebanese Sign Language (LSL) gestures in real-time and translates them into multiple spoken and written languages using natural language processing. The project addresses a critical gap in accessibility tools for LSL users who face communication barriers with non-signers. As the lead AI engineer, I was responsible for developing the gesture recognition model, optimizing real-time inference performance, and integrating the computer vision pipeline with the NLP translation module. The system combines state-of-the-art deep learning models for gesture recognition with language translation capabilities to deliver accurate, low-latency translations. Omnisign won the Public Choice Award at the National FYP Demo Day 2025, recognizing its social impact and technical excellence in creating accessible AI solutions that bridge communication gaps for underserved communities.
problem: The deaf and hard-of-hearing community in Lebanon lacked accessible tools for real-time communication with non-signers, limiting social integration and access to services.
solution: Built a computer vision and NLP pipeline that recognizes Lebanese Sign Language gestures and translates them into multiple spoken and written languages in real-time.
myRole: Lead AI engineer responsible for gesture recognition model development, real-time inference optimization, and CV-to-NLP pipeline integration.
outcome: Won Public Choice Award at National FYP Demo Day 2025, enabling real-time communication for Lebanese Sign Language users with measurable social impact.
features: Real-time Lebanese Sign Language gesture recognition using deep learning
Multi-language translation output (Arabic, English, French)
Computer vision-based gesture interpretation with 90%+ accuracy
NLP-powered translation module for natural language output
Low-latency inference optimized for real-time communication
Award-winning accessibility innovation (Public Choice Award, National FYP Demo Day 2025)

title: Autonomous Race Car - WRO 2023 Champion
description: Fully autonomous vehicle built in 20 days that won the World Robot Olympiad Future Engineers 2023 competition using computer vision and sensor fusion.
longDescription: This project showcases a fully integrated autonomous vehicle developed from scratch in only 20 days for the World Robot Olympiad (WRO) Future Engineers 2023 competition. The challenge required building a self-driving car capable of navigating a race track with traffic signs, obstacles, and dynamic pathing under strict time constraints. As technical lead, I designed a dual-processor architecture: a Jetson Nano handling high-level computer vision tasks (lane detection, traffic sign recognition) and an Arduino Mega managing deterministic real-time control using PID steering stabilization and sensor fusion from IMU and color sensors. This separation ensures robust performance by dedicating the Jetson to perception and the Arduino to low-latency motor control. The system achieves smooth autonomous cornering, accurate traffic sign detection, and dynamic path planning. Built with OpenCV for computer vision, C++ for Arduino control, and Python for Jetson processing, the project demonstrated excellent teamwork, rapid prototyping under pressure, and reliable embedded AI performance. The vehicle successfully won the WRO Future Engineers 2023 competition, completing autonomous navigation challenges with 95%+ accuracy, validating the robustness of the dual-MCU architecture and sensor fusion approach for real-time autonomous systems.
problem: Build a self-driving car capable of autonomous navigation with traffic sign recognition and obstacle avoidance in under 3 weeks for the WRO Future Engineers 2023 competition.
solution: Designed dual-processor system: Jetson Nano for computer vision (lane/sign detection) + Arduino Mega for real-time motor control with PID steering and IMU-based sensor fusion.
myRole: Technical lead - designed system architecture, implemented OpenCV-based computer vision pipeline, integrated sensor fusion, and coordinated team development.
outcome: Won WRO Future Engineers 2023 championship - vehicle navigated autonomous challenges with 95%+ accuracy, validating dual-MCU architecture for real-time autonomous systems.
features: End-to-end autonomous vehicle built in 20 days
Dual-MCU architecture: Jetson Nano (perception) + Arduino (control)
Real-time lane detection and traffic sign recognition using OpenCV
PID-based steering control with IMU and color sensor fusion
95%+ navigation accuracy in competition environment
Modular design for expandability and future enhancements
WRO Future Engineers 2023 Champion

title: AI-Powered Smart Desk for Classrooms
description: IoT desk with computer vision-based posture tracking and automatic ergonomic adjustments for enhanced learning and health outcomes.
longDescription: The Smart Learning Table is an interactive, sensor-driven workstation designed to improve student engagement, ergonomic comfort, and learning outcomes in classroom and office environments. Poor posture and static desk configurations contribute to health issues and reduced focus during long study or work sessions. As lead engineer, I designed and built an IoT system using ESP32 microcontrollers for hardware control, motorized actuators for automatic height and tilt adjustment, and an OpenCV-based computer vision system for real-time posture tracking. The desk monitors user posture using a camera and provides immediate visual feedback through LED indicators when poor posture is detected. It automatically adjusts height and tilt to maintain optimal ergonomic positioning based on user height and activity. The system supports multiple control interfaces, including a web dashboard for analytics and Bluetooth controllers for manual adjustments. I developed the posture tracking computer vision model, integrated the hardware control system, and built the web dashboard for real-time monitoring and historical analytics. The project was successfully deployed as a university prototype, monitoring posture for 30+ students in a classroom setting and demonstrating the potential of IoT and AI for human-centered design in educational environments.
problem: Students and office workers suffer from poor posture and ergonomic issues during long study or work sessions, leading to health problems and reduced focus.
solution: Built IoT desk system with ESP32 controllers, motorized actuators, and OpenCV posture analysis that automatically adjusts desk height/tilt and provides real-time posture feedback.
myRole: Lead engineer - designed system architecture, implemented OpenCV posture tracking model, developed web dashboard, and integrated hardware control systems.
outcome: University prototype successfully deployed in classroom - monitored posture for 30+ students with real-time feedback and ergonomic adjustments, demonstrating IoT + AI for educational environments.
features: Automated motorized height and tilt adjustment based on user profile
Real-time posture tracking using OpenCV and computer vision
Immediate visual feedback via LED indicators for posture correction
Web dashboard for real-time monitoring and posture analytics
Bluetooth controller interface for manual adjustments
IoT connectivity with data logging for ergonomic insights
Successfully deployed in classroom environment with 30+ students

title: 360° Spherical Mobile Panorama
description: A professional computer vision pipeline for creating seamless 360° spherical panoramas from phone footage.
longDescription: A robust computer vision tool that reconstructs seamless 360° spherical panoramas from standard phone photos or videos. It utilizes advanced algorithms like ORB feature matching and RANSAC for precise alignment, handles geometric modeling for pure rotation, and automatically generates an interactive web-based 360° viewer using Three.js.
problem: Creating high-quality 360° panoramas from handheld devices is challenging due to alignment errors, parallax issues, and the need for complex stitching of multiple frames.
solution: Built a Python pipeline that uses feature detection, robust matching, and spherical projection to seamlessly stitch images. Integrated temporal smoothing for video inputs and created a Three.js viewer for immediate immersive playback.
outcome: Developed a professional-grade tool capable of generating 8K+ panoramas suitable for VR and real estate, with optimized video processing and memory management.
features: Automatic Video/Photo Stitching
Robust Feature Matching (ORB)
Spherical Projection Mapping
Interactive Web Viewer
Video Keyframe Extraction
8K+ Resolution Support
Memory Optimized Processing
